text.title: FinOps OPERATE Phase — Governance and Cost Gates Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each budget MUST have at least three alert thresholds: 50% (notify), 80% (warn-with-owner), 100% (page-on-call). Single-threshold alerts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single 100% alerts arrive too late; tiered alerts give owners time to react before overage.

rule.statement: IaC pull requests MUST run a cost-diff gate (Infracost or equivalent) and break the build if monthly impact exceeds the team's PR-cost threshold (default $500).
rule.rationale: Post-merge cost reviews are too late; merging is the action that creates cost.

rule.statement: Dev/test environments MUST have idle-detection + auto-stop after the configured inactivity window (default 4h). Re-start is a single button.
rule.rationale: Manual non-prod scheduling decays; idle-detection + auto-stop is the stable form.

rule.statement: All FinOps policies (tag-mandatory, max-instance-size, region-allowlist, max-budget) MUST be declared as code (OPA, AWS Config, Azure Policy, Pulumi Policy Packs).
rule.rationale: UI-clicked policies drift; code-declared policies are reviewable, testable, version-controlled.

rule.statement: Monthly budget variance >5% (over or under) MUST trigger a post-mortem with documented root cause and corrective action.
rule.rationale: Under-budget often hides idle resources; over-budget is obvious; both need explanation.

rule.statement: Skip when prerequisites unmet: INFORM/OPTIMIZE incomplete, no IaC, or no budget owners — OPERATE will misfire on noisy data.
rule.rationale: OPERATE is a maturity step, not a starting point; without upstream foundations it fires false positives.
